text:
What if AI isn’t just a tool but a whole new way of working for marketers?

Cognizant's marketing team is finding out, with a plan to go t beyond using AI and redesign every workflow and reskill every member of the team: https://t.co/r1YJjtGGzv https://t.co/3lVVkZDJSq
name: Cognizant

text:
When ChatGPT broke in late 2022, every new tool started with "AI-powered".

This month's editorial pick of 8 fresh tools tells a different story.

Not one calls itself an AI tool. They're a cart recovery tool, a loyalty platform, a prospecting workflow. 🧵 https://t.co/XHkU2SwT9Q
name: RankmyAI - the world’s largest AI directory

text:
Using one AI tool for everything is why your workflow feels clunky. 

Some are built for research, some for copy, some for coding. A mismatch makes the output look robotic. 

It’s not about using MORE AI, it’s about using it smarter

Love all things AI? Check out the link in bio! https://t.co/Z7Nn1ZlWhf
name: Digital Laura Anderson

text: AI won't replace you, but someone using AI will. The secret to staying ahead is curiosity: start experimenting with one new tool today to future-proof your workflow. #AITools #FutureOfAI
name: Kes Karim

text:
The most rational-sounding decision is also the most dangerous: "Let's wait and see how AI settles."

I get it. You don't want to invest time in a tool that might disappear.

But the real gap isn't between Tool A vs Tool B. It's between those using *any* AI workflow and those https://t.co/6Zd0akejpG
name: Paul

text:
I just shipped a major Synkora refresh. 🧵

Most AI tools solve a slice of the problem. A framework. A chatbot widget. A workflow tool.

We built the missing layer — the full platform for teams who need agents in production, not just in demos.

What's new:

→ New branding + https://t.co/EWyX9psRAp
name: Raju Mazumder

text:
